title: Divide multi-panel Rig position-ramp amplitude
goal: Dissolves/Divide (15.40 dB) active phase (f00-f13) is UNIFORMLY 12-14 dB while the settle (f14-f20) is 19.18 dB. DECODE: Divide.motr has ZERO keyframes; 18 Shape panels each carry a Fade In/Fade Out behavior + a Rig (49 Rig Behaviors) driving scale(100/105 x16), position(100/101 x15), opacity(200/202 x6), Colorize remap(2/353/103 x8) via 18 static snapshots ramped by transition progress. EVIDENCE the panels translate-apart TOO LITTLE: mid-row (y=540) A/B boundary cols at f0013 are engine=[19,243,299,472] vs GUI-GT=[16,247,292,623,797] — GT's right panel group separates out to col ~623/797, engine stops at ~472. So the position-snapshot ramp amplitude (or its progress mapping) for the panels is systematically short across the whole active window. FIRST STEP: decode the 15 position(100/101) Rig snapshots' start/end values from Divide.motr and trace them through applyRigBehaviors/getSnapshotValue in engine/src/evaluator/links.ts — check whether the snapshot's authored 'value' (target separated position) is being reached at progress=1 or being under-ramped/clamped. Likely a snapshot ramp-endpoint or progress-mapping fix generic to Rig-driven static-snapshot panels (must fire on >=2 built-ins for no-hardcode). This is a subsystem-sized change; too big for one net-positive opportunistic tick.
